steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: In B(t) = 80(2)^t, the input t is time in days and the output B(t) is the predicted bacteria count. B(4) = 1,280 means: plug in t = 4, get 1,280. The question asks which choice correctly labels which value is the time and which is the count.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '4 days after first measured, predicted count was 1,280': t = 4 days is the input, B(4) = 1,280 cells is the output. This matches the function correctly. ✓
  • Choice B — '4 days after first measured, predicted count was 16': Uses the correct input t = 4 days, but claims the output is 16 cells. B(4) = 80 · 24 = 1,280, not 16. ✗
  • Choice C — '1,280 days after first measured, predicted count was 4': Swaps t and B(t) — 1,280 is the output, not the time. ✗
  • Choice D — '1,280 days after first measured, predicted count was 16': Also swaps the roles; 16 has no basis in B(4) = 1,280. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
